name: KrillApp.Trigger.CronTimer
title: Cron Timer
description: Fires on a recurring schedule defined by a cron expression. When the schedule fires, the cron node succeeds and every node observing it (listing it in their sources) is invoked. Supports 6-field format (SEC MIN HOUR DOM MON DOW — e.g. '*/5 * * * * *' = every 5 seconds) and legacy 5-field format (MIN HOUR DOM MON DOW). The server polls every second. Supports wildcards (*), ranges (1-5), steps (*/5), and lists (1,3,5). The name meta field is required on the wire.
shortDescription: Fires on a cron schedule; nodes observing it are invoked.
llmPurpose: Fires on a recurring schedule defined by a cron expression, invoking every node that observes it as a source.
llmBehavior: server polls the cron expression every second
on fire, the node succeeds and its observers are invoked
llmInputs: cron expression string — 6-field 'SEC MIN HOUR DOM MON DOW' (e.g. '0 0 20 * * *' for daily at 8pm) or legacy 5-field 'MIN HOUR DOM MON DOW'
llmOutputs: fires on schedule; all observing nodes are invoked
llmTypicalUseCases: run automation at a specific time daily
llmCreationHints: Create a CronTimer when the user wants time-based recurring execution. Requires a valid cron expression — 6-field SEC MIN HOUR DOM MON DOW ('*/5 * * * * *' = every 5 seconds); the legacy 5-field MIN HOUR DOM MON DOW form is also accepted. The name meta field is required on the wire. Wire actions to observe the timer: list it in their sources with SOURCE_INVOKED.

llmConnectionHints.role: schedule trigger whose observers are invoked each time it fires
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observed-side: each action lists this timer in its sources with SOURCE_INVOKED (parent-default wiring covers nodes dropped under it); the timer itself needs no sources.

llmExamples: Create a cron timer that runs every day at 8pm.
Schedule this maintenance task for Sunday at midnight.
Trigger this polling sequence every 15 minutes.
